items.title: REGENXBIO bets on FDA flexibility with filing plans for Duchenne gene therapy
items.snippet: A few short days after announcing an FDA pivot on a separate asset, REGENXBIO is planning to test the agency’s apparent newfound rare disease outlook on another late-stage gene therapy.

items.title: BIO CEO Crowley, DOD doctor Overton are top candidates for FDA commissioner: RBC
items.snippet: A new report from RBC Capital Markets lists Biotechnology Innovation Organization chief executive John Crowley and military physician Heidi Overton as potential candidates for the role of FDA commissioner—in addition to the agency’s current acting leader, top food regulator Kyle Diamantas.

items.title: FDA hiring 2,200 people to staff up after last year’s DOGE cuts
items.snippet: The FDA has so far secured 600 new hires and is looking for 1,600 more as interim leadership at the agency aims to rebuild the workforce and morale after more than a year of intense attrition.

items.title: Novartis, Antares stars align in cancer deal that could top $1.9B
items.snippet: Novartis and Antares Therapeutics are shooting for the stars, launching a joint mission to develop small molecule oncology therapies.

items.title: Lilly deepens relationship with China’s Abbisko for $1.9B biobucks
items.snippet: Lilly has already spent more than $25 billion in potential business development commitments this year, including the $6.3 billion buyout of Centessa Pharmaceuticals that closed today.

items.title: AbbVie keeps M&A rolling, FDA’s reversal train chugs along, BIO 2026 dispatch, more
items.snippet: AbbVie scooped up immunology player Apogee Therapeutics for nearly $11 billion in one of the year’s top deals to-date, while Sanofi made a big play to survive its upcoming Dupixent patent cliff; FDA uncertainty continues as the agency changes direction on gene therapies by uniQure and REGENXBIO; and Jef Akst and Annal...

items.title: Serapha takes reverse merger route to Nasdaq with $230M and gene editor from China
items.snippet: The combined business entity with Boundless Bio, which will carry Serapha Bio’s name and fold in Boundless Bio, will focus on the development of a gene editor for alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency.

items.title: Takeda enters transitional year as new CEO seeks return to growth
items.snippet: For the 2026 fiscal year, Takeda anticipates declines in revenue and profit, highlighting what CEO-elect Julie Kim says is the need for the company to “invest in future growth.”

items.title: BIO 2026: US needs to dodge China’s ‘inescapable dependencies’ to protect biotech
items.snippet: Speaking to media on Tuesday, BIO CEO John Crowley complimented China’s rise as a biotech powerhouse but said U.S. policy needs to protect and maintain America’s lead.

items.title: Ollin parlays Vabysmo beat into $330M raise to test eye disease drug in Phase 3
items.snippet: With positive feedback on a Phase 3 trial design, Ollin Biosciences has collected a $330 million series B to advance its VEGF/Ang2 antibody, which bested Genentech’s therapy in a head-to-head study.

items.title: The most dangerous person in the company
items.snippet: Controlling CEOs, manipulative middle managers and high-performing jerks can damage employees’ trust in employers and motivate them to hit the job market. Kaye/Bassman’s Michael Pietrack discusses the problematic behaviors executives must watch for in the workplace.

items.title: Big Pharma restructures to ride out ‘existential risk’
items.snippet: Even biopharma’s biggest players have been forced to take a hard look at their businesses and realign their cost structures to cope with the continued and compounding challenges plaguing the industry.

items.title: FDA uncertainty adds risk to biopharma deals
items.snippet: While merger and acquisition activity has been robust of late, frequent changes in guidance and leadership at the regulator add risk to any transaction.

items.title: Sangamo slashes 51 jobs amid bankruptcy filing
items.snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

items.title: Lilly, Astellas circle Sangamo assets as biotech files for bankruptcy
items.snippet: As Sangamo runs out of cash, Eli Lilly and Astellas have emerged as stalking horse bidders for key assets, including a Fabry gene therapy currently being submitted for potential FDA approval.

items.title: Merck posts ‘encouraging’ Phase 3 ulcerative colitis data but Roche challenge looms
items.snippet: Given its intravenous route of administration, Merck’s tulisokibart will likely “need to be meaningfully improved” over Roche’s afimkibart, which can be given subcutaneously, BMO Capital Markets analysts said. Both assets are being tested for ulcerative colitis.

items.title: Lilly’s tirzepatide will be biggest blockbuster ever, topping $70B by 2032: Evaluate
items.snippet: Eli Lilly’s weight-loss franchise—including the tirzepatide products Mounjaro and Zepbound, and the weight-loss pill Foundayo—is projected to account for nearly half of the total sales of the top 10 drugs in 2032.

items.title: Can ADCs be kinder and gentler? Join BioSpace at BIO to find out
items.snippet: While existing antibody-drug conjugates have been incredible advancements in cancer care, patients are ready for a better experience. At the BIO International Convention in San Diego on Thursday, BioSpace will lead a discussion on the next generation of ADCs.

items.title: Pfizer delivers ‘disappointing’ late-stage lung cancer data for ADC from $43B Seagen buy
items.snippet: Despite an overall survival miss, Leerink Partners said Pfizer’s antibody-drug conjugate showed “promising” signals of efficacy in a subgroup of patients who had undergone only one prior line of treatment.

items.title: BIO 2026: Biopharma dealmakers seek to protect ‘premium pricing’ from Trump’s MFN policy
items.snippet: With drug pricing now embedded in U.S. policy, business development teams in biotech and pharma are changing the way they strike deals, including acknowledging policy uncertainties with renegotiation clauses.

items.title: BIO 2026: Cell and gene therapy sector must break the mold to thrive
items.snippet: If cell and gene therapy makers are going to achieve their mission to improve patients’ lives, the industry must come together to share information across stakeholders, from regulators to manufacturers to payers.

items.title: At FDA, ‘caretaker mode’ is better than power trip mode
items.snippet: UniQure’s planned third-quarter submission for its Huntington’s disease gene therapy may be a harbinger of a more flexible FDA under acting commissioner Kyle Diamantas—but how long will it last? And how can companies be sure these positive decisions won’t just be reversed?

items.title: Lilly just bought renowned regulator Peter Marks a pipeline to play with
items.snippet: FDA veteran Peter Marks will now shape the future of Eli Lilly’s vaccines work after the buys of Curevo, LimmaTech Biologics and Vaccine Company for up to $3.8 billion total.

items.title: 3 top FDA, CDC roles filled by 15 different people in less than 18 months
items.snippet: The Department of Health and Human Services is spinning its wheels, unable to establish steady leadership at three major divisions—the CDC and the FDA’s two primary review units.
